expressions.english: tardy student
to be late
to be late for school
expressions.notes:
학교 = school
지각하다 = to be late

expressions.english: to be late for work
You are late again.
Don't be late.
Why were you late today?
I think I will be late today.
He is always late.
expressions.notes: 매날 = every day; all the time

expressions.english: You should never be late for your job interview.
expressions.notes:
면접 = (job) interview
절대 = never
-(으)면 안 되다 = shouldn't; must not

expressions.english: You must not be late today.

expressions.korean: 어쩔게 오늘 같은 날 지각할 수 있어요?
expressions.english: How could you be late on a day like today?
expressions.notes:
어쩔게 + (으)는 수가 있어요? = How could you + V?; How dare you + V?
N + 같은 날 = a day like + N
-(으)는 수 있다 = can, to be able to; there is a chance that + S + V

expressions.english: I overslept today and was late for school.
expressions.notes: 늦잠을 자다 = to get up late, oversleep, sleep in

expressions.korean: 오늘 아침에는 차가 막혀서 지각을 했어요.
expressions.english: I was late this morning because of traffic.
expressions.notes:
아침 = morning; breakfast
차가 막혀다 = the traffic is bad; the road is jammed with traffic

expressions.korean: 저는 이번 달에 벌써 세 번이나 지각했어요.
expressions.english: I have already been late (for work/school) three times this month.
expressions.notes:
이번 = this; this time
달 = month
벌써 = already
세 번 = three times
다섯 번 = five times
한 번 = once, one time
결석하다 = to be absent; to not go to a class/school
똑같다 = to be the same; to be equal

expressions.korean: 다섯 번 지각하면 한 번 결석한 것과 똑같아요.
expressions.english: Being late five times is counted as one absence.
expressions.notes:
친구 = friend
만나다 = to meet (up)
-기로 하다 = to plan to + V; to decide to + V; to make a promise to + V
분 = minute

expressions.english: I arranged to meet my friend, but I was 30 minutes late.
expressions.notes:
택시 = cab, taxi
-데도 = even though, although

expressions.english: Even though I went to work by taxi, I was 10 minutes late.

expressions.korean: 저는 학교 다닐 때 한 번도 지각을 한 적이 없어요.
expressions.english: When I was in school, I was never late (for school).
expressions.notes:
다니다 = to go to (a place); to attend
-(으)ㄹ 때 = when/while + S + V
한 번도 + 없다 = haven't not + V + even once
-(으)ㄴ 적이 없다 = to have never p.p.

expressions.korean: 내일은 중요한 시험이 있으니까 지각하면 안 돼요.
expressions.english: There is an important exam tomorrow, so you can't be late.
expressions.notes:
내일 = tomorrow
중요하다 = to be important
중요한 is the adjective form of 중요하다.
시험 = test, exam
